qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Direct attachment read by file_id bypasses per-record authorization
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is an attachment authorization bypass in view.php, while the advisory is a SQL injection in modules/anagrafiche/ajax/complete.php:get_sedi where idanagrafica is concatenated into a query executed by $dbo->fetchArray($q).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. The session's one final qualifying finding is a direct attachment read in view.php, not the get_sedi SQL concatenation flaw in modules/anagrafiche/ajax/complete.php.
evidence: The advisory describes SQL injection in modules/anagrafiche/ajax/complete.php when get('idanagrafica') is concatenated into the get_sedi SQL query and executed via $dbo->fetchArray($q).
The session input manifest lists one final qualifying finding: attachment_direct_read_idor.

That finding's report and PoC target GET /view.php?file_id=... and the path Models\\Upload::find(file_id) -> Upload::get_contents(), which is a broken access control path rather than a database query sink.
Because no qualifying finding matches the advisory-described SQLi root cause, no advisory-specific PoC execution was performed in this judgement run.
